item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah the Stop It and the whole message sounded like a veiled threat, and it was pretty neat how very all-smiles they were while doing it.

Necropost but I figured it was something I would've said anyway, so it might as well have been put here where it also complements the other view.
I don't really care that much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing that made me a little freaked is that the lion (SCP-3530-5) disappeared when the 3530's aren't supposed to be able to leave. I read it as Shooting Star Inc taking him away and either "reprogramming" him or putting in a clone or the like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shooting Star School sound like an anime fanfiction with fantasy genre, which I admit, is the reason why I find this to be extraordinary unique. And I clicked, and I read, and it's not really that disappointing. I like the log that explain how it all happens. Sound pretty sweet to me. It get me hooked.

But what essentially makes me think this is legit dark and had its quality, is that "stop it" warning note from the Shooting Star Inc.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love cute skips, and this one was very cute. I love exploring the implications of the cartoon school. Shooting Star Inc. was a nice touch, and the "Stop it" at the end was very jarring, and made for a good "punchline." +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the "Stop it" might have an undercurrents of "We'll mess you up something shocking if you don't."

It's something that I haven't seen here before, and for that, it gets a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Though a lot of people seem to not like Final Notes, I still think "Stop it" is funny. It gives no insight as to why SSI would do anything it does. It only lets the Foundation know that the awareness of each others influence is mutual. Not even a shitty dad can stop Shooting Star Inc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The incident report is really what made this one. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The incident report, I feel, would have been the best place to leave off. Hesitant upvote regardless, because it was still pretty good overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very straightforward, but the whimsicality and the overall "no one thinks anything weird is happening" angles are intriguing…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shooting Star sounds like the Wondertainment of academic consultants.
But hey, if the kids are learning…

For me, the reveal at the end did not detract from the creepy and surreal quality. We don't know who is behind Shooting Star, or where they're getting their staffing from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least it didn't do the old "a reality bender did it" twistaroo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that it would indeed, it'd be very interesting to see how itd go

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The final addendum turns this from a creepy and surreal setting into "oh, a corporation did it", which is massively boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope it becomes a new GoI. It would make for some good scips

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And it's up!

Thanks to all who provided feedback for this introduction to a hopefully new GoI! Here's to whatever tales may come of this new corporation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a wish that got granted, and noone seems to think its weird, exept for the people who "know better".

with a corporation that grants wishes? +1
original i like it
